event: purulent drainage from sinuses on the scalp behind her right ear
oxygen saturation of 98% on room air
large fluctuant mass over the occipital and parietal portions of the scalp
purulent drainage over the occipital and parietal portions of the scalp
posterior auricular mass without signs of active drainage
extensive multifocal scalp swelling along the right temporal, right posterior parietal and left frontal regions
admitted to the intensive care unit
incision and drainage of scalp lesion
paranasal sinus CT with intravenous contrast did not show involvement
transesophageal echocardiogram without evidence for endocarditis
returned to the operating room for further pulse irrigation of the wounds
scalp wound ultimately measured 20 cm in length, 10 cm in width and 2 cm depth
right posterior auricular wound measured 7×7×2 cm
wound bed free of purulent drainage
25% of the patient’s scalp was affected
wounds of the scalp and post-auricular region were definitively closed with split-thickness skin grafts
split-thickness skin grafts harvested from the right thigh
Versajet used to clean the wound bed prior to placement of skin graft
split-thickness skin graft taken from left thigh and meshed with 1:1.5 dermacarrier
100% take of the grafts on postoperative day 7
plan to complete a full 7-week course of intravenous Vancomycin
no areas of the graft were rejected
